summary: iter240j-ext FRONTEND ONLY — UserNameLink (UNL) applied at 4 call sites. SOURCE REVIEW confirms UNL is correctly wired in ServicesPage.js L758 (marketplace vendor), CrowdfundingModule.js L182 ('par <UserNameLink>'), CommentSection.jsx L127 (feed comment author), ReelsPage.js L109 (reel comment @username). E2E TESTS (Bob bob@japap.com / Test1234! after solving math captcha): (1) REELS comments UNL: PASS — opening reel comments panel reveals 4 UNL spans, clicking '@username' navigates to /profile/bob48c0 cleanly. (2) MARKETPLACE vendor UNL: BUG — 30 UNL with data-testid='user-name-link' are correctly rendered inside each product card (scoped query [data-testid^='product-'] [data-testid='user-name-link'] returns 30). However, clicking the vendor UNL navigates to /marketplace/p/<product_id> (the parent <Link> destination) instead of /profile/<username>. ROOT CAUSE: UserNameLink calls e.stopPropagation() but NOT e.preventDefault(); the marketplace card parent is <Link to=...> which renders as <a href=...>. stopPropagation halts React synthetic propagation but the browser's NATIVE default click-to-navigate on the parent <a> still fires. (3) CROWDFUNDING UNL: NOT VERIFIED — /crowdfunding times out on networkidle; /services?view=crowdfunding loads but renders 0 cards / 0 UNL / 0 'par ' text matches even after 8s. Either Bob's projects aren't visible there, the view tab requires extra interaction, or the slug differs. (4) FEED comments UNL: NOT VERIFIED — feed comment toggle testid pattern is 'comment-btn-post_<id>' (not 'feed-comments-toggle-' as guessed); my script didn't try the right prefix, so I couldn't expand any comment thread. Source review confirms UNL is wired correctly at CommentSection.jsx L127, but live click flow needs retest. REGRESSION: Marketplace card click on the price area still navigates to /marketplace/p/<id> (works). Reels comment open buttons (7 found) still work.

ui_bugs.issue: When UNL is nested inside a React Router <Link> (i.e. an <a href>), clicking the UNL calls navigate('/profile/...') but the parent <a>'s native href-navigation ALSO fires immediately because e.stopPropagation() does NOT prevent the browser's default behavior on the ancestor <a>. Result: user lands on the parent Link's destination (e.g. /marketplace/p/prod_xxx) instead of /profile/<handle>. This breaks the Marketplace vendor click (and would also break any other usage nested in <Link>). Fix: in UserNameLink.onClick, also call e.preventDefault() before navigate(). Reels works because its parent is a <div>, not <Link>.

integration_issues.issue: Tested URL /services?view=crowdfunding and /crowdfunding — first renders 0 cards (cf-project-card-<slug>) after 8s, second times out on networkidle. Could not verify owner_name UNL click. Possible causes: Bob has no public CF project visible without filter, or the CF tab requires an explicit click on a tab inside ServicesPage, or fetch is gated. Recommend manual verification by main agent.

Source code at CommentSection.jsx L127 is correctly wrapping the author name with <UserNameLink>. Live UI test could not expand any comment thread because the comment toggle testid is 'comment-btn-post_<id>' (not the prefix I tried). Once comments are expanded, the UNL should work BUT only if the comment list is NOT itself wrapped in a <Link>/<a> with onClick navigation (verify after fixing the preventDefault bug above).

action_items: HIGH: Update /app/frontend/src/components/common/UserNameLink.jsx — in the onClick handler, also call e.preventDefault() before navigate(). Otherwise UNL nested inside a <Link>/<a> (Marketplace, possibly Crowdfunding card if wrapped) loses navigation to /profile/ because the parent <a> href fires natively.

MEDIUM: Verify Crowdfunding rendering at /crowdfunding and /services?view=crowdfunding for Bob — make sure projects load and the 'par <UserNameLink>' actually renders. If CF cards are wrapped in <Link>, the same preventDefault fix is required.
MEDIUM: Verify Feed comment author UNL click works after the preventDefault fix. Comment expansion testid is 'comment-btn-post_<id>'.
LOW (DX): Consider adding a dedicated data-testid to the UNL when used in each context (e.g. user-name-link-vendor, user-name-link-cf, user-name-link-comment) for deterministic E2E.
critical_code_review_comments: UserNameLink.jsx onClick only calls e.stopPropagation() (when stopPropagation=true) but never calls e.preventDefault(). When the wrapper element is a React Router <Link>, the inner span click still triggers the native <a> href navigation, defeating the purpose of UNL. The fix is one line: `if (stopPropagation) { e.stopPropagation(); e.preventDefault(); }` (or always preventDefault since the span itself has no default behavior, this is safe).

UserNameLink renders a <span role='link'> but inside a parent <a>, this produces invalid HTML (nested interactive elements). Functional fix above is sufficient, but consider switching parents from <Link> to <div onClick={navigate}> to avoid the anti-pattern.

success_rate.frontend: ~40% — Reels UNL PASS (1/4); Marketplace UNL rendered but click broken due to preventDefault bug (1/4 partial); Crowdfunding and Feed UNL clicks UNVERIFIED due to load / selector issues (2/4 not tested)

test_credentials: bob@japap.com / Test1234! ; captcha solved live via [data-testid=math-captcha-question] + [data-testid=math-captcha-answer]
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public preview: https://japap-refactor.preview.emergentagent.com. Captcha must be solved live: read math-captcha-question text, parse 'a op b =', fill math-captcha-answer. Marketplace vendor UNL bug: parent <Link to='/marketplace/p/...'> in ServicesPage.js L703 — UNL inside it (L758) needs e.preventDefault() in onClick to actually navigate to /profile. Reels UNL works because parent is <div>. Feed comment toggle prefix is 'comment-btn-post_'. Crowdfunding direct route /crowdfunding hangs on networkidle (use domcontentloaded); /services?view=crowdfunding renders 0 cards for Bob — investigate.
rca_of_the_issue: The Marketplace UNL navigation failure RCA: in UserNameLink.jsx L21-24, onClick does `if (stopPropagation) e.stopPropagation(); navigate('/profile/...')`. stopPropagation halts React synthetic event bubbling, but the click is dispatched on a <span> nested inside a <Link>/<a>. The browser's NATIVE event flow still reaches the <a>, and the <a>'s default action (follow href) fires. navigate() runs first (pushing /profile to history), then the <a> default kicks in immediately and replaces it with /marketplace/p/<id>. Adding e.preventDefault() in UNL's onClick will suppress the native default and the navigate() call will stick. Reproduction: login as Bob, go /services?view=marketplace, click any seller name under a product card — lands on the product page (wrong). Mitigation (1 line): `onClick = (e) => { if (stopPropagation) e.stopPropagation(); e.preventDefault(); navigate(...); }`. This is safe for non-anchor parents too because <span> has no default action.
